title: Invalid Dequeue Operation from Queue using Array

statement.markdown: Given a queue implementation using an array, write a function to dequeue an element. If the queue is empty, return -1. If the front element is not removed properly, subsequent dequeue operations will result in incorrect output.
statement.inputFormat: The function will be called with a queue size and an array of integers.
statement.outputFormat: The output should be the dequeued element, or -1 if the queue is empty.
statement.notes: Please ensure that the function handles the case when the queue is empty correctly.

examples.explanation: Dequeue the front element (1) from the queue.

hints.text: Handle the case when the queue is empty correctly.

editorial.title: Invalid Dequeue Operation from Queue using Array
editorial.intuition: The function should remove the front element from the array and return it.
editorial.bruteForce: Remove each element one by one and return it.
editorial.betterSolution: Use a pointer to track the front and rear of the queue.
editorial.optimalSolution: Use an array and a pointer to track the front and rear of the queue.
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: The function should return the correct dequeued element for all valid inputs.
editorial.dryRun: Example run of the function with a sample input.
commonMistakes: Incorrectly handling the case when the queue is empty
Not removing the front element from the array correctly
